Scenario.title: C0015: Multi-Stage Cyber Espionage Against Transportation Infrastructure
Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Complex A sophisticated cyber campaign targeting transportation systems employs AI-driven reconnaissance, custom C2 protocols, and multi-stage exploitation to compromise network infrastructure, extract sensitive data, and disrupt operations.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Open Source Intelligence (OSINT), Active Directory, Internet-facing assets, OS Process, Windows Registry, Process, Network Traffic, Public IP/Network Block, Domain Name Registrar, File, Malware Artifact, Process
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Artificial Intelligence, Custom Command and Control Protocol, Delivery Method, Code Injection, Windows Registry Key Creation (Mechanism), OS API Execution (Mechanism), Process Injection, Network Traffic Content (Mechanism), Network Service Scanning, Pass the Hash, File Modification (Mechanism), Encrypted Channel, Process Creation (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attackers deploy AI algorithms to analyze public data sources, mapping transportation networks and identifying vendor ecosystems to pinpoint critical infrastructure components.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.technique_name: T1094: Custom Command and Control Protocol
stage.description: Compromised cloud services are repurposed to establish covert C2 infrastructure, leveraging Active Directory for persistent communication channels.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: Spear-phishing emails with malicious attachments are crafted to exploit human factors, delivering payloads to endpoints within the transportation network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: Malicious code is injected into legitimate processes to execute payloads without requiring direct user interaction.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: A scheduled task is created in the Windows Registry to maintain persistent access post-compromise.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: Attackers manipulate access tokens to elevate privileges and gain administrative control over systems.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: Malware is embedded within legitimate processes to evade detection mechanisms.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.capec: CAPEC-80: Using UTF-8 Encoding to Bypass Validation Logic
stage.description: Credentials are extracted from network traffic by encoding data to bypass standard validation checks.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: Scans are conducted on public IP ranges to map internal network structures and locate high-value targets.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.capec: CAPEC-461: Web Services API Signature Forgery Leveraging Hash Function Extension Weakness
stage.description: Stolen credentials are used to move laterally across domains, exploiting hash-based authentication weaknesses.

stage.name: Collection
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: Sensitive operational data is collected from decommissioned servers and stored files.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: Data is exfiltrated via encrypted channels to avoid detection by firewall monitoring tools.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-670: Software Development Tools Maliciously Altered
stage.description: Malicious tools are deployed to encrypt critical transportation control systems, causing operational disruption.

Scenario.summary: C0015 executes a complex, multi-phase attack on transportation systems, combining AI-driven reconnaissance, custom C2 infrastructure, and advanced exploitation techniques to achieve long-term persistence, data exfiltration, and operational impact.
